title: Developer-focused snippet viewer for InstaNotificationService auto-reply code

requirements.summary: Add a page that renders the Android Manifest service declaration and Kotlin NotificationListenerService code as labeled, syntax-highlighted blocks with one-click copy per snippet.
acceptanceCriteria: The page shows two clearly labeled sections: one for the AndroidManifest.xml snippet and one for the Kotlin service class snippet.
Each snippet is rendered in a monospace code block with preserved whitespace/indentation.
Each snippet has a "Copy" control that copies the exact snippet text to the clipboard.
All user-facing labels and helper text are in English.

file_operations.description: Add frontend dependencies needed for syntax highlighting (e.g., Prism) and ensure the build includes them for client-side highlighted rendering.
Create the app root that renders the required snippet viewer page as the primary UI (no Android functionality; display-only).
Implement the snippet viewer page with two clearly labeled sections (AndroidManifest.xml and Kotlin service class) and English helper text.

Create a reusable code-snippet block component that preserves whitespace/indentation, applies syntax highlighting, and provides a one-click Copy button using the Clipboard API to copy the exact raw snippet text.
Ensure the app mounts the new App component and continues to import the global stylesheet required for the snippet viewer UI.

requirements.summary: Apply a coherent developer-oriented theme across the snippet viewer (layout, typography, code block styling, and button styling) without blue/purple as primary colors.
acceptanceCriteria: The UI uses a consistent, developer-oriented aesthetic (e.g., neutral/dark or warm monochrome) applied to headers, backgrounds, code blocks, and buttons.
Theme is applied consistently across the page layout and interactive elements.

file_operations.description: Define global theme tokens and base styles for a developer-focused aesthetic (neutral/dark or warm monochrome), ensuring primary accents avoid blue/purple and styling is consistent for headers, backgrounds, code blocks, and buttons.
Adjust/extend Tailwind theme mappings (via existing CSS-variable color system) to align with the new non-blue/purple primary palette and consistent spacing/typography for the snippet viewer.
Apply the theme consistently in the page layout (container spacing, headings, section chrome) and ensure interactive controls (Copy buttons) match the developer-oriented styling.
Ensure code block visuals (background, border, font, line-height) and Copy button styling match the global theme and remain consistent across both snippets.
